title: 3D Models
summary: Lazy-loaded Three.js viewer that reads its colours from the palette.
content:
The model-viewer shortcode embeds a Three.js scene inside a panel. Three.js is loaded from jsdelivr via ESM import map — only on pages that use it.

Scene colours read from --base00, --base05, and --base0D at init. A MutationObserver on <html> keeps them in sync when the palette changes live.

Demo scene

No src attribute, so this renders a procedural torus-knot + ring using palette colours. Drag to orbit.

Demo
(drag to orbit)
  • Background matches --base00.
  • Knot is tinted with --base0B (green).
  • Ring uses --base0E (purple).
  • Lights blend --base05 and --base0D.

Loading a model

Pass a src URL for a glTF asset:

{{< model-viewer src="/models/scene.glb"
                title="Scene"
                height="420" >}}

The Three.js import is memoised, so multiple viewers on one page download the library once. The camera auto-frames the loaded object’s bounding box.

title: Mermaid Diagrams
summary: Flowcharts and sequences coloured by the site palette.
content:
Mermaid is loaded from CDN only on pages that use it. The palette is piped in from the site’s base16 colorscheme, so diagrams re-theme automatically when you swap data/colorscheme.yaml.

Flowchart

flowchart LR
  A[Start] --> B{Ready?}
  B -- yes --> C[Render]
  B -- no --> D[Wait]
  D --> B
  C --> E[Done]

Sequence

sequenceDiagram
  participant Browser
  participant Hugo
  participant Chroma
  Browser->>Hugo: request /blog/syntax-highlighting/
  Hugo->>Chroma: highlight ```go block
  Chroma-->>Hugo: classed s
  Hugo-->>Browser: HTML

title: Panels
summary: Every titled container in the theme is one parametric partial.
content:
Every titled container on this site is partials/panel.html called with a different variant. Pass a dict with title, number, variant, and content:

{{ partial "panel.html" (dict
  "title"   "Posts"
  "variant" "list"
  "items"   .Pages
) }}

Plain

Freeform HTML in a titled border. Hover to see the border and header highlight together.

About

A titled container with freeform HTML content. The title sits on the top border.

List + Preview

A list panel paired with a preview panel. Clicking an item in the list swaps the preview content without a page navigation.

[1] Items
  • First itemMarch 1, 2025
  • Second itemFebruary 14, 2025
  • Third itemJanuary 5, 2025
[2] First item

This is the preview for the first item. Click a different entry in the list panel to swap the content here.

On real list pages (like the blog index), list-preview.js fetches index.json once and swaps from memory. This demo uses inline content instead.

Second item selected. The preview title updates and the list panel counter tracks which item is active.

The scroll indicator on the right edge of each panel updates as content changes height.

Third item. On pages without JavaScript the list items are plain links that navigate to the full page — the preview is a progressive enhancement.

On real section pages (like the blog index) list-preview.js fetches index.json once and renders from memory. Without JavaScript the list items are plain links to the full pages.

Tabs

Dual-mode: tab switchers in the border when narrow, side-by-side columns when wide. Resize the window to see both states.

Tabs
Narrow-Wider-Keyboard
(resize window)

Below the width threshold, tab names in the border act as switchers.

Once wide enough, all tabs render side-by-side as columns.

Focus a tab and use ArrowLeft/ArrowRight to navigate.

Input

A prompt-style wrapper. The border and title switch to --base0C on focus and the textarea auto-grows as you type.

Script
(auto-grows)

title: Layouts
summary: Page skeleton and a small family of responsive grid classes.
content:
Every page is nav + main + footer inside a centred .container (max 1500px). main holds either a single panel or a grid of panels.

Grid classes

All grids collapse to a single column at 768px.

.grid-2col

Left column.

Right column.

.grid-3col

Collapses to two columns at 992px, then one at 768px.

One

Two

Three

.grid-main-sidebar

Sidebar

1fr.

Main

2fr.

.grid-auto-fit

As many 300px-min columns as the container allows.

Card

Card

Card

Card

.grid-master-stack

Master

2fr.

Stack 1

Stack 2

Stack 3

.grid-monocle

Full-width

Single-column grid with consistent gap.

Second panel.

Special-case grids

  • .grid-list-preview — blog and taxonomy lists. Scrollable title list (300px min) + preview pane (2fr).
  • .home-grid — homepage. 2fr intro + 1fr recent posts.

title: Nav
summary: Chevron-clipped bar with a title tab and link pills.
content:
A flex row of clip-path chevron shapes. Title tab on the left, menu pills on the right. No JavaScript.

Plain

Title
  • Home
  • Blog
  • About

With icons

Title icon from site.Params.titleIcon; link icons from each menu entry’s params.icon.

 tui
  • Home
  • Blog
  • Projects
  • About

Active state

Current section gets --base0B. Hover any pill to see --base0C.

Demo
  • Home
  • Projects
  • Blog
  • About

Responsive

992px
Pill padding shrinks.
768px
Link text hides, leaving icon-only pills. Overflows scroll horizontally.

Resize this window to see both states.

Config

[params]
  titleIcon = "terminal"

[menu]
  [[menu.main]]
    name = "Home"
    url = "/"
    weight = 10
    [menu.main.params]
      icon = "home"
  [[menu.main]]
    name = "Blog"
    url = "/blog/"
    weight = 20
    [menu.main.params]
      icon = "document"

title: Footer
summary: Copyright left, social links right, icon-only on mobile.
content:
The footer sits at the bottom of every page via partials/body/footer.html. Copyright on the left, social links on the right, separated by |.

Default

© 2025 Site Title
GitHub | LinkedIn | RSS

With icons

Each link can carry a glyph from data/icons.yaml. Below 768px the text hides, leaving icon-only links.

© 2025 Your Brand
GitHub | Twitter | Email | RSS

Icons only

Drop the link text for a compact row of glyphs.

© 2025 Portfolio
 |  |  | 

Config

Links come from params.social in hugo.toml:

[params]
  copyright = "© 2025 tui demo"
  [[params.social]]
    name = "GitHub"
    url = "https://github.com/user"
    icon = "github"
  [[params.social]]
    name = "RSS"
    url = "/index.xml"
    icon = "rss"

External URLs open in a new tab; internal URLs (starting with /) stay in-page. Omit copyright to default to © {year} {site.Title}.

title: Tinted Images
summary: Monochrome images tinted to any base16 colour with CSS mask-image.
content:
The tinted-image shortcode recolours monochrome images (SVG, PNG) to any palette slot using CSS mask-image. No JavaScript, no approximation — the colour is an exact CSS custom property.

Single colour

{{< tinted-image src="/images/terminal.svg" color="base0D" width="64px" height="64px" >}}

Palette sampler

The same image in every accent colour:

Different shapes

Any monochrome image works — opaque pixels become the tinted colour, transparent pixels stay transparent.

How it works

The image acts as an alpha mask on a coloured <div>:

.tinted-image {
  background-color: var(--base0D);
  mask-image: url('/images/logo.svg');
  mask-size: contain;
  mask-repeat: no-repeat;
}

Opaque pixels → colour shows. Transparent pixels → nothing renders. Swap the palette and every tinted image updates instantly.

Parameters

src
Path to a monochrome image (required)
color
Base16 slot name, e.g. base0D (default: base0D)
alt
Accessible label
width
CSS width (default: auto)
height
CSS height (default: auto)

Best results with images that are solid white (or any opaque colour) on a transparent background. SVGs work perfectly; PNGs need an alpha channel.

title: Icons
summary: Nerd Font glyphs looked up by name from a single data file.
content:
Icons are Nerd Font glyphs mapped in data/icons.yaml. The theme ships a default set; override by placing your own data/icons.yaml in the site root.

Usage

{{ partial "icon.html" "github" }}
{{ partial "icon.html" (dict "name" "search" "class" "icon--lg") }}

Unknown names render as the raw codepoint, so you can also pass a literal hex value.

Available glyphs

 arrow-left
 arrow-right
 bars
 book
 bookmark
 calendar
 chevron-down
 chevron-left
 chevron-right
 chevron-up
 clock
 code
 cog
 comment
 css
 document
 download
 email
 error
 external-link
 file
 folder
 github
 gitlab
 go
 heart
 home
 html
 info
 java
 javascript
 link
 linkedin
 lua
 mastodon
 phone
 privacy
 python
 rss
 ruby
 rust
 search
 star
 success
 tag
 tags
 terminal
 twitter
 typescript
 upload
 user
 users
 warning

Adding icons

Add entries to data/icons.yaml as name: codepoint pairs:

docker: f308
kubernetes: fd31

Codepoints are hex without a 0x prefix. Browse available glyphs at nerdfonts.com/cheat-sheet .

title: Syntax Highlighting
summary: Code blocks coloured via Chroma, mapped to the base16 palette.
content:
Chroma emits CSS classes (the theme sets markup.highlight.noClasses = false), and _chroma.scss maps those classes to var(--base0E), var(--base0B), var(--base09), and friends. Swap the site’s data/colorscheme.yaml and every token colour updates in lock-step with the rest of the page.

 Go

package main

import "fmt"

// greet prints a welcome message.
func greet(name string) {
    fmt.Printf("Hello, %s!\n", name)
}

func main() {
    greet("world")
}

 Python

def fib(n):
    """Return the n-th Fibonacci number."""
    a, b = 0, 1
    for _ in range(n):
        a, b = b, a + b
    return a

 Rust

use std::collections::HashMap;

fn count_words(text: &str) -> HashMap<&str, usize> {
    let mut counts = HashMap::new();
    for word in text.split_whitespace() {
        *counts.entry(word).or_insert(0) += 1;
    }
    counts
}

 Shell

# Build and serve the example site
hugo server --themesDir ../.. \
  --navigateToChanged \
  --noHTTPCache

Inline & prose

You can also drop inline code into a paragraph — it uses var(--base0A) on the var(--base01) surface, so it stands out without clashing. External links like Hugo’s docs open in a new tab via the render-link.html hook; internal links stay in-page.

“The purpose of computing is insight, not numbers.” — Richard Hamming

Tables

Lang Stable Year
Go yes 2012
Rust yes 2015
Zig no 2016

title: Theme
summary: Palette and typography flow from a single data file.
content:
Every colour on this site comes from the sixteen CSS custom properties emitted by data/colorscheme.yaml. Swap the YAML, rebuild, done.

Preview a different palette

Pick a scheme and watch the whole page re-tint in place. Reload to restore the built-in palette.

Current palette

base00
base01
base02
base03
base04
base05
base06
base07
base08
base09
base0A
base0B
base0C
base0D
base0E
base0F

To ship your own palette, drop a data/colorscheme.yaml in your site root:

name: My Palette
scheme:
  base00: "1F1F28"
  # …
  base0F: "D27E99"

Hex values without a leading # (standard base16 format).

Typography

Font family
FiraCode Nerd Font, ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace
Base size
15px
Line height
1.6

TTF files ship in static/fonts/, loaded with font-display: swap. FiraCode ligatures render in code blocks, and the Nerd Font patches provide the icon glyphs used throughout the UI.
